title: Transactions, wallets, and signing
description: A transaction is a signed message ordered by an account nonce; the private key holder authorizes everything, and signatures must be replay-protected.
body: Use when handling transaction submission or off-chain signatures. An account is a key pair; a transaction is signed with the private key and carries a per-account nonce that enforces order and prevents replay on the same chain. Off-chain signatures (EIP-712 typed data) authorize actions without a transaction but must include a domain separator, nonce, and deadline or they can be replayed. Do not reuse a signature without replay protection, and never expose or hardcode a private key. Actions: include nonce/deadline and chain id in signed messages and verify the recovered signer. Validation signals include a replay test where a reused signature is rejected. Risk boundary: a missing nonce or domain separator lets attackers replay a signature to drain approval; treat unprotected signatures as a critical defect.
